title: unbroker DPA adapter
description: A national data-protection authority adapter. One JSON file per DPA under references/dpa/. The loader (scripts/dpa.py) validates every adapter against this schema at load time; a malformed adapter is rejected, not silently ignored.

id.description: Short slug. Must match one of the dpa values in scripts/dossier.py:RESIDENCY_LEGAL_FRAMEWORK.

name.description: Full official name of the authority (in the original language).

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (e.g. IT, FR, DE, GB).

language.description: Primary complaint language (ISO 639-1). DPAs often accept English, but filing in the national language speeds response.

web_form_url.description: URL of the DPA's online complaint form. Browser-form automation target.

complaint_template.description: Relative path to the templates/dpa-complaints/<id>.txt file. Default: 'dpa-complaints/<id>.txt' if omitted.

generic_ok.description: Explicitly true only when this adapter intentionally uses the generic Article 77 template instead of a DPA-specific template.

expected_days.description: Statutory response window (Art. 78(2) GDPR: 3 months for the controller's response; DPA complaint timelines vary). Used by the recheck queue to know when to follow up.

email.description: Direct email (when the web form is unavailable or for an additional paper trail).

phone.description: For DPAs that accept voice complaints (rare; usually only for accessibility).

notes.description: Free-text quirks (e.g. 'requires PEC for Italian residents', 'must attach a copy of ID for first-time filers').

examples.name: Garante per la protezione dei dati personali
examples.notes: Italian residents should file via PEC (protocollo@pec.gpdp.it) for the strongest paper trail; web form is available as fallback.
